notes: The three cartridges playable on the mess hall's slowstick console this summer.  Northwind Harbor is in Sam's duffel from the start.  Earthman Chronicles and Fey Faire live beside the console on the shelf · Jenny checks them in and out.  Booting each grants a fact + a scrapbook entry.  A hero-scene JSON at res://.../sprites/scenes/cart_<id>.json renders the title screen when the cart boots.

carts.title: NORTHWIND HARBOR
carts.shelf_note: In Sam's duffel from home.  Sam has finished Chapter 1.  Chapters 2 and 3 are 'damaged' on the ROM · a repeating tone at 220 Hz and a black screen.  Sam has learned not to boot past Chapter 1.
carts.blurb: A puzzle-adventure set in a fictional fishing town on the Oregon coast.  Chapter 1 is a boy walking his uncle's dog through the harbor at 5:47 AM · gathering a set of small everyday objects · the game is very quiet and very kind.  Nobody Sam knows has ever played Chapter 2.  Two people online claim to have.  Both accounts are dubious.
carts.on_boot_line: You slot Northwind Harbor.  The title theme plays · fourteen seconds of soft slowstick pad in F major.  You know it by heart.  You back out before Chapter 1's boy asks his uncle's dog if he's ready to walk.  Sam has heard that question enough times this year.

carts.title: THE EARTHMAN CHRONICLES
carts.shelf_note: Adaptation of a lost 1948 Hubbard manuscript · not officially in his bibliography.  Sam's dad found this one at an estate sale in '86.  The dad said 'don't play it drunk' and didn't explain.  Sam has not asked follow-up questions.
carts.blurb: It is the seventeenth of January, nineteen forty-six, in Pasadena.  Doctor Jack Whiteside stands in his home laboratory before a specific ritual chalked on the floor.  He is thirty-two.  He has a rocket to test in the morning.  He has a book he has been reading for six years.  He has a friend named RAFATON who will scribe the working.  Rafaton takes notes with his left hand.  Jack does not notice the left hand.  Beyond the north window, the San Gabriels are visible against a still sky.  This is the last thing Jack sees before Parsa.
carts.on_boot_line: You slot The Earthman Chronicles.  A ten-second musical sting that sounds like the New World Symphony run through a broken monitor.  A title card that reads 'A LOST WORK · ADAPTED FOR THE SLOWSTICK · MARCH 1985.'  A watercolor cover of a red desert under two moons.  A small figure in a Caltech tie stands with his back to camera.  You get to a menu · NEW GAME / LOAD / A THIRD OPTION IN A LANGUAGE YOU DO NOT KNOW.  You back out.  It was already a lot and you have not started.

carts.title: FEY FAIRE
carts.shelf_note: A late-Rev-2 Oneironautics game · Ines Rocha's dialogue lead credit.  A camp donation from Jenny personally.  Jenny does not check it out to kids under eleven.  Sam is eleven this summer.  Barely.
carts.blurb: A traveling fairy carnival sets up in a nameless Willamette Valley suburb every seven years.  Children who wander in do not, all of them, come out.  You play a twelve-year-old whose older sister went in in 1976 and has not aged since.  The carnival is not evil · it is just not obligated to human time.  Six endings.  Rocha's early register.  Sold poorly.  Loved dearly by the few.
carts.on_boot_line: You slot Fey Faire.  A title card in cream and old-rose · handwritten letters, the F's serifed the way Rocha serifs her F's.  A sound like a music box a note off from itself.  A girl at a booth with too many prizes and an offer that isn't quite a question.  You back out before you say yes.  You take the cart off the console anyway.  You want to think about it before you go back in.
